1.title: Open Beam Vertical Deflection- Threshold Configuration Panel

content.text: System shall open the Beam Vertical Deflection (B1) — Threshold Configuration panel when the user selects the B1 test from the Rules and Actions → Testing tab.
open the Beam Vertical Deflection (B1) threshold configuration panel
I can configure allowable deflection parameters for beam testing.
the user is on Rules and Actions → Testing tab
the user selects the B1 test
the system shall open the Beam Vertical Deflection (B1) — Threshold Configuration panel.
System shall display the panel title “Beam Vertical Deflection (B1) — Threshold Configuration”.
I can clearly identify the configuration being edited.
the system shall display the title “Beam Vertical Deflection (B1) — Threshold Configuration.”
System shall display the Local scope indicator beside the panel title.
I understand the scope of the configuration.
the system shall display a Local scope indicator beside the panel title.
System shall display a description indicating the configuration defines allowable deflection parameters for beams using formula-based span-dependent logic.
see a description of the configuration
I understand the logic used to calculate beam deflection.
the system shall display a description indicating the configuration defines allowable deflection parameters for beams using formula-based span-dependent logic.
System shall display the Deflection Modes section within the panel.
I can understand the different beam deflection evaluation conditions.
the system shall display the Deflection Modes section.
System shall display the Elastic Mode (Service Condition) formula as Δ_allow = L / [Elastic Divisor].
I understand how service condition deflection is calculated.
the Deflection Modes section is visible
the system shall display the formula Δ_allow = L / [Elastic Divisor].
System shall provide an editable Elastic Divisor input field.
I can control allowable beam deflection limits.
the Elastic Mode configuration is displayed
the system shall provide an editable Elastic Divisor input field.
System shall set the default value of the Elastic Divisor to 200.
the Elastic Divisor field is displayed
the system shall set the default Elastic Divisor value to 200.
System shall validate the Elastic Divisor to accept integer values between 100 and 1000.
the Elastic Divisor input to be validated
only acceptable configuration values are entered.
the user enters a value in the Elastic Divisor field
the value is outside the valid range
the system shall validate that only integer values between 100 and 1000 are accepted.
System shall display the Permanent Mode (Residual Condition) formula as Δ_allow = L / [Permanent Divisor].
I understand the calculation for residual deflection.
the Deflection Modes section is displayed
the system shall display the formula Δ_allow = L / [Permanent Divisor].
System shall provide an editable Permanent Divisor input field.
I can define allowable residual deflection limits.
the Permanent Mode configuration is displayed
the system shall provide an editable Permanent Divisor input field.
System shall set the default value of the Permanent Divisor to 1000.
the system starts with recommended configuration values.
the Permanent Divisor field is displayed
the system shall set the default value to 1000
System shall validate the Permanent Divisor to accept integer values between 200 and 2000.
only valid configuration values are accepted.
the user enters a value in the Permanent Divisor field
the system shall accept integer values between 200 and 2000 only.
System shall display a note indicating that allowable deflection is calculated automatically based on beam span (L).
see how deflection values are calculated
I understand how beam span affects allowable deflection.
the Deflection Modes section is displayed
the system shall display a note indicating that allowable deflection is calculated automatically based on beam span (L).
System shall display the Escalation Multiplier section.
I can define severity escalation thresholds.
the system shall display the Escalation Multiplier section.
System shall display the escalation rule Red when Δ > Multiplier × Δ_allow.
I understand when a Red severity is triggered.
the Escalation Multiplier section is displayed
the system shall display the rule Red when Δ > Multiplier × Δ_allow.
System shall provide an editable Red Threshold Multiplier input field.
the Escalation Multiplier section is displayed
the system shall provide an editable Red Threshold Multiplier input field.
System shall set the default value of the Red Threshold Multiplier to 1.5.
the system shall set the default Red Threshold Multiplier to 1.5.
System shall validate the Red Threshold Multiplier to accept numeric values between 1.0 and 3.0 with decimal values allowed.
the user enters a value in the multiplier field
the system shall accept numeric values between 1.0 and 3.0 including decimals.
System shall display a note indicating that the multiplier defines escalation from Amber to Red severity.
I know how severity changes occur.
the Escalation Multiplier section is displayed
the system shall display a note indicating that the multiplier defines escalation from Amber to Red severity.
System shall display the Calculation Preview section.
I can understand how configuration values affect results.
the system shall display the Calculation Preview section.
System shall display an example span value (e.g., 2700 mm) in the calculation preview.
the Calculation Preview section is displayed
the system shall display an example span value (e.g., 2700 mm).
System shall display an Elastic Mode example calculation showing the allowable deflection value and corresponding Red limit.
see example calculations for Elastic Mode
the Calculation Preview section is displayed
the system shall display the Elastic Mode allowable deflection value and Red limit.
System shall display a Permanent Mode example calculation showing the allowable deflection value and corresponding Red limit.
see example calculations for Permanent Mode
the Calculation Preview section is displayed
the system shall display the Permanent Mode allowable deflection value and Red limit.
System shall update the Calculation Preview automatically when divisor or multiplier inputs change.
the calculation preview to update automatically
I can immediately see the impact of configuration changes.
the Calculation Preview section is displayed
the divisor or multiplier values change
the system shall automatically update the calculation preview.
System shall display a Cancel button allowing the user to discard changes and close the configuration panel.
I can exit without saving updates.
the system shall discard changes and close the panel.
System shall display a Save Changes button allowing the user to save updated threshold configuration values.
the updated parameters are applied to the system.
the system shall save the updated threshold configuration values.
System shall display a Close (X) icon allowing the user to close the panel.
close the configuration panel using the header icon
I can quickly exit the configuration view.
the user clicks the Close (X) icon
the system shall close the panel.
System shall return the user to the Rules and Actions → Testing tab when the panel is closed.
return to the Testing tab after closing the panel
I can continue managing testing configurations.
the system shall return the user to Rules and Actions → Testing tab.
